title: Authors and Citation
text: Frigaard M (2026). rsixer: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/mjfrigaard/Rsixer.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rsixer: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo},   author = {Martin Frigaard},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/mjfrigaard/Rsixer}, }

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: modern Shiny application demonstrating R6 object-oriented design patterns building modular, maintainable applications. Rsixer improved version tooltipexplorer showcases: R6 Classes Shiny Modules - Replaces traditional function-based modules R6 objects Namespace Adaptation - Uses shiny::NS() within R6 classes proper module isolation Reactive State Management - Demonstrates dependency injection reactive composition Five Tooltip Backends - Compares bslib, shinyhelper, prompter, shinyalert, reactable Financial Data Integration - Uses real stock market data tidyquant

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: Shiny module now R6 class : $new(id) Constructor - Creates module instance unique namespace $ui() Public Method - Builds returns module’s UI $server() Public Method - Initializes server logic returns reactive values Private Fields - Manages internal state, namespace functions, private reactives Example:
code: # In app_ui() inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs") download <- ModDownload$new(id = "download")  bslib::page_sidebar(   sidebar = inputs$ui(),   outputs$ui() )  # In app_server() inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") inputs_r <- inputs$server()  outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs") perf_r <- outputs$server(inputs_r = inputs_r)  download <- ModDownload$new(id = "download") download$server(inputs_r = inputs_r, perf_r = perf_r)

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: Five different approaches demonstrated across tabs:

what: Use R6 Modules in Your Own Apps
title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
code: library(rsixer)  # Create module instances inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "my_inputs") outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "my_outputs")  # Build UI ui <- page_sidebar(   sidebar = inputs$ui(),   outputs$ui() )  # Build server server <- function(input, output, session) {   inputs_r <- inputs$server()   perf_r <- outputs$server(inputs_r = inputs_r) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
code: library(rsixer)  # Fetch stock prices prices <- get_stock_prices(   tickers = c("AAPL", "MSFT"),   from = "2023-01-01" )  # Compute returns returns <- get_stock_returns(prices)  # Performance metrics perf <- summarise_performance(returns)  # Rolling volatility vol <- compute_rolling_vol(returns, window = 30)

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
code: Rsixer/ ├── R/ │   ├── mod_inputs.R           # ModInputs R6 class │   ├── mod_outputs.R          # ModOutputs R6 class │   ├── mod_download.R         # ModDownload R6 class │   ├── app_ui.R               # Top-level app_ui() function │   ├── app_server.R           # Top-level app_server() function │   ├── launch.R               # launch() convenience wrapper │   ├── mod_tooltip.R          # mod_tooltip() UI helper │   ├── mod_hoverinfo.R        # mod_hoverinfo() server helper │   ├── utils_data.R           # Data utility functions │   ├── utils_logging.R        # Logging utilities │   └── utils_operators.R      # %||% operator ├── tests/testthat/ │   ├── test_mod_inputs.R      # Unit tests for ModInputs │   ├── test_mod_outputs.R     # Unit tests for ModOutputs │   ├── test_mod_download.R    # Unit tests for ModDownload │   ├── test_utils_data.R      # Tests for data utilities │   ├── test_utils_logging.R   # Tests for logging utilities │   ├── test_mod_tooltip.R     # Tests for tooltip helper │   └── test_mod_hoverinfo.R   # Tests for hoverinfo helper ├── inst/ │   └── report_template.Rmd    # R Markdown report template ├── man/                       # roxygen2 documentation (auto-generated) ├── DESCRIPTION ├── README.md (this file) └── LICENSE

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: module maintains namespace prevent ID conflicts:
code: ModInputs <- R6::R6Class("ModInputs",   public = list(     initialize = function(id = "inputs") {       private$id <- id       private$ns <- shiny::NS(id)  # Create once     },     ui = function() {       # Use private$ns() throughout       shiny::selectizeInput(         inputId = private$ns("tickers"),         ...       )     }   ),   private = list(     id = NULL,     ns = NULL   ) )

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: Parent modules pass reactives child modules via method arguments:
code: # In app_server inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") inputs_r <- inputs$server()  outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs") perf_r <- outputs$server(inputs_r = inputs_r)  # Pass dependency

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: Private methods encapsulate reactive creation transformation:
code: ModOutputs <- R6::R6Class("ModOutputs",   public = list(     server = function(inputs_r) {       shiny::moduleServer(private$id, function(input, output, session) {         # Private reactive methods         private$prices_reactive()         private$returns_reactive()         private$performance_reactive()       })     }   ),   private = list(     prices_reactive = function() { shiny::eventReactive(...) },     returns_reactive = function() { shiny::reactive(...) },     performance_reactive = function() { shiny::reactive(...) }   ) )

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
code: # Verbose development mode app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # Quiet production mode app_set_log_threshold(logger::WARN)

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: R6 Classes: Unit tests instantiation, UI generation, namespace isolation Data Utilities: Tests price/return calculations, performance metrics Logging: Tests error handling logging behavior Helpers: Tests mod_tooltip() mod_hoverinfo()

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: Core: - shiny ≥ 1.7.0 - Web framework - bslib ≥ 0.5.0 - Bootstrap 5 layouts - R6 ≥ 2.5.0 - Object-oriented programming UI/Tooltips: - bsicons - Bootstrap icons - shinyhelper - Help modals - prompter - Hover tooltips - shinyalert - Modal alerts - reactable - Interactive tables Data: - dplyr - Data manipulation - tidyquant - Stock data - slider - Rolling window functions - scales - Number formatting Infrastructure: - logger - Structured logging - glue - String interpolation - htmltools - HTML generation - rmarkdown - Report generation

title: R6-Based Shiny Application Demo
text: “Failed retrieve data” warnings startup occurs Yahoo Finance unavailable ’s network issue. app automatically falls back realistic demo data, functionality preserved. OpenMP Library Error: symbol found '___kmpc_barrier' indicates broken gower package binary (OpenMP dependency issue). Fix rebuilding source:
code: # Check connectivity curl::curl_fetch_memory("https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/")  # Or test tidyquant directly tidyquant::tq_get("AAPL", get = "stock.prices") # Clear the problematic cache system("rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/org.R-project.R/R/renv/cache/v5/macos/R-4.5/x86_64-apple-darwin20/gower")  # Reinstall with source compilation renv::restore(rebuild = TRUE)

what: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: thin wrapper around logger::log_threshold() applies chosen level every logger namespace used rsixer.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: level logger log-level object, e.g. logger::DEBUG, logger::INFO (default), logger::WARN.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: Invisibly returns level.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: Log levels lowest highest verbosity: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, SUCCESS, WARN, ERROR, FATAL. default threshold INFO — TRACE DEBUG lines silent production. Internally, lapply() iterates namespace vector calls logger::log_threshold() entry.  return value lapply() discarded; level returned (invisibly).

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Verbose output during development app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # Quiet production mode app_set_log_threshold(logger::WARN) } # }

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: server-side helper formats hover/tooltip content reactable table cells.  Wraps display value htmltools <span> title attribute, browsers render native tooltip hover.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
code: mod_hoverinfo(   type = "reactable",   contents = character(0),   display = NULL,   size = NULL,   style = NULL,   ... )

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: type Back-end target.  Currently "reactable". contents character string (named character vector) used tooltip text title attribute.  Named vectors produce "Name: value" pairs joined " | ". display value render visibly inside cell.  Passed child node <span>.  Kept explicit parameter never accidentally matched size positional argument order. size CSS font-size span wrapper, e.g. "0.8rem". NULL (default) leaves unchanged. style Inline CSS span wrapper, e.g. "cursor:help". NULL (default) applies none. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <span> tag.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: htmltools <span> tag title attribute set. Use inside reactable::colDef(cell = ..., html = TRUE) renderer.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ reactable::colDef(   name = "Ann. Return (%)",   html = TRUE,   cell = function(value, index) {     mod_hoverinfo(       type     = "reactable",       contents = paste0("Annualised log return: ", value, "%"),       display  = paste0(value, "%"),       style    = "color:#198754; cursor:help"     )   } ) } # }

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: lightweight UI helper wraps several tooltip/popover/modal back-ends behind single, consistent interface.  Drop anywhere inside UI another module — paired server function required.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"),   type = c("bslib", "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert"),   contents = "",   size = NULL,   style = NULL,   helper_type = c("inline", "markdown"),   helper_size = c("m", "s", "l"),   alert_type = c("info", "success", "warning", "error"),   ... )

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text:
trigger shiny.tag (plain text) tooltip attaches . Defaults small bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"). type Back-end use. One "bslib" (default), "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert". contents Character string vector — tooltip/popover/modal body. HTML accepted "bslib". "shinyhelper" plain-text character vector preferred — elements joined
helper(type = "inline"), embedding HTML tags causes double-escaping. Ignored plain text "prompter". "shinyalert" HTML supported (rendered via html: true). size CSS font-size trigger wrapper span, e.g. "0.85rem". NULL (default) leaves font size unchanged. Note: purely CSS property wrapper — distinct helper_size (shinyhelper modal size). style Additional inline CSS trigger wrapper, e.g. "color:#6c757d". NULL (default) applies none. helper_type Content type forwarded shinyhelper::helper() type argument. One "inline" (default) "markdown". Ignored back-ends. helper_size Modal size forwarded shinyhelper::helper() size argument. One "s", "m" (default), "l". Ignored back-ends. Kept separate size avoid two params colliding. alert_type Alert style forwarded shinyalert() type argument. One "info" (default), "success", "warning", "error". Ignored back-ends. ... Extra arguments forwarded directly underlying back-end function: "bslib" → bslib::popover() (e.g. title, placement) "shinyhelper" → shinyhelper::helper() (e.g. title, colour, icon, buttonLabel, easyClose, fade) "prompter" → prompter::add_prompt() (e.g. position, rounded, bounce, arrow, animate) "shinyalert" → stored data-sa-* attributes; recognised keys title confirmButtonText

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: shiny.tag (shiny.tagList) ready embed UI.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: type = "shinyalert" requires delegated jQuery click handler injected page. See app_ui() example.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # bslib popover (click to open) mod_tooltip( type = "bslib", contents = "A bslib popover with HTML.", title = "More info" ) # shinyhelper inline help modal mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("Sharpe Ratio"), type = "shinyhelper", contents = c("Sharpe Ratio", "Assumes a zero risk-free rate."), helper_type = "inline", helper_size = "m", title = "Sharpe Ratio" ) # prompter attribute-driven tooltip mod_tooltip( trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"), type = "prompter", contents = "Number of trading days in the rolling window.", position = "right" ) # shinyalert modal on click (requires sa-handler in app_ui) mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("What is ann. vol?"), type = "shinyalert", contents = "Annualised volatility = SD of daily log returns x sqrt(252).", title = "Annualised Volatility", alert_type = "info" ) } # }

what: Summarise Performance Metrics by Ticker — summarise_performance
title: Summarise Performance Metrics by Ticker — summarise_performance
text: Computes annualised return, annualised volatility, Sharpe ratio (assuming zero risk-free rate) ticker. Validates input handles edge cases (zero volatility, missing returns).

title: Summarise Performance Metrics by Ticker — summarise_performance
Summarise Performance Metrics by Ticker — summarise_performance
text: returns tibble returned get_stock_returns().

title: Summarise Performance Metrics by Ticker — summarise_performance
text: tibble columns: symbol, ann_return, ann_vol, sharpe.

what: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: Wraps expr tryCatch() : logs warnings via logger::log_warn() re-issues Shiny can also handle logs errors via logger::log_error() re-throws caller / Shiny sees error normally

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: expr Expression evaluate (passed unevaluated via ...). context Short string identifying call site, e.g. "mod_outputs / prices_r".  Prepended every log message. ns Logger namespace string. Defaults "rsixer/app".

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: value expr success; re-throws error.

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- with_logging(   context = "my_module / compute",   ns = "rsixer/app",   sqrt(4) ) } # }
